title: Bridge Essay: Brain Neural Underpinnings of Interoception and Decision-Making in Alzheimer's Disease: A Narrative Review.
concept: Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

**Brain Neural Underpinnings of Interoception and Decision-Making in Alzheimer's Disease: A Narrative Review.** - Front Neurosci (2022). DOI: 10.3389/fnins.2022.946136. PMID: 35898412. This study reviews recent literature on interoception directing decision-making in Alzheimer's disease (AD). According to the somatic marker hypothesis, signals from the internal body direct decision-making and involve the ventromedial prefrontal cortex (vmPFC). After reviewing relevant studies, we summarize the brain areas related to interoception and decision-making (e.g., vmPFC, hippocampus, amygdala, hypothalamus, anterior cingulate cortex, and insular cortex) and their roles in and relation
